data.title: Navigator launches joint venture to build two ammonia gas carriers
data.description:
Navigator Holdings (NYSE:NVGS) +1.9% pre-market Thursday after saying it formed a joint venture with Amon Maritime to construct two 51,530 cubic meter ammonia-fueled carriers at an average cost of $84M per vessel.

Each vessel project received a NOK 90M (~$9M) grant from the Enova Norwegian government agency; the JV will finance the majority through commercial bank financing, with remaining capital contributed by Navigator (NYSE:NVGS) and Amon Maritime.

The vessels will operate under five-year time charters with a "blue-chip industry leader," with deliveries scheduled for June and October 2028, the companies said.

Navigator (NVGS) will own ~80% of the Navigator Amon Shipping joint venture, with Amon Maritime holding the remaining ~20%.

data.title: Bit Origin secures $500 million equity and debt facilities to launch Dogecoin treasury
data.description:
• Bit Origin (NASDAQ:BTOG) announced on Thursday that it has entered into agreements with accredited investors for the sale of up to $400 million in Class A ordinary shares and up to $100 million in convertible debt to launch the company’s Dogecoin treasury strategy.
  • The company has completed an initial closing of $15 million under the convertible debt facility and intends to use a significant portion of the proceeds for its initial Dogecoin acquisition.
  • BTOG +27.78% premarket to $0.424.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Globavend to execute 1-for-200 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Globavend Holdings (NASDAQ:GVH) plans to implement a 1-for-200 reverse stock split of the company’s ordinary shares.
  • The company anticipates that the ordinary shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a post-split basis upon the opening of the market on July 21, 2025.
  • GVH --13.6% premarket to $0.0635
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. acquires Equinox Agency
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE:AJG) announced on Thursday the acquisition of Emmaus, Pennsylvania-based Equinox Agency. 
  • The company did not disclose terms of the transaction.
  • Equinox is an employee health and benefits consulting firm serving businesses in Pennsylvania's Lehigh Valley. The Equinox team, led by Thomas Groves and Dan Gagnier, will remain in their current location under the direction of Scott Sherman, head of Gallagher's Northeast region employee benefits consulting and brokerage operations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Citizens Financial Q2 earnings aided by fees, NII; delivers Q3 guidance
data.description:
Citizens Financial (NYSE:CFG) on Thursday turned in Q2 earnings and revenue that topped Wall Street expectations, buoyed by "strong NII and fee growth, disciplined expense management, and credit results that are trending favorably,” said Chairman and CEO Bruce Van Saun. 

Q2 2025 underlying EPS of $0.92, beating the $0.88 average analyst estimate, climbed from $0.77 in the prior quarter and $0.82 a year earlier.

Q2 total revenue of $2.04B, also surpassing the $2.01B consensus, rose from $1.94B in Q1 and $1.96B in Q2 2024.

CFG stock gained 0.9% in premarket trading.

“We saw some sizable M&A advisory fees push out to July, but offset that with strong performance across other fee categories," Saun noted.

NII accelerated 3% sequentially and 2% from a year ago to $1.44B in Q2.

Noninterest income rose to $600M from $544M in the earlier quarter and $553M in Q2 2024.

Provision for credit losses advanced to $164M in Q2 from $153M in Q1, but slid from $182M in last year's Q2.

Period-end loans and leases of $139.3B in Q2 vs. $137.6B in Q1. Period-end deposits of $175.1B in Q2 vs. $172.7B prior.

For Q3, the bank expects NII growth of 3%-4% from Q2's $1.44B; noninterest income rising in the low-single-digits from Q2's $600M and noninterest expense edging up ~1%-1.5% from Q2's $1.32B. It maintained its full-year 2025 guidance provided in January.

Conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: IQSTEL reports 1H 2025 preliminary revenue of $128.8 million
data.description:
• IQSTEL (NASDAQ:IQST) on Thursday announced 1H 2025 preliminary unaudited revenue of $128.8 million. June alone contributed $27.3 million, a significant increase from May's $23.7 million.
  • Starting July 1st, IQSTEL will begin consolidating revenue from its newly acquired subsidiary, Globetopper, expected to add $5 million to $6 million per month in additional revenue.
  • On this trajectory, IQSTEL expects to reach a $400 million annualized revenue run rate during Q3 — several months ahead of the company's original year-end target. This milestone represents a critical leap forward in the company's strategic roadmap toward achieving its $1 billion revenue goal by 2027.
  • IQST -0.03% premarket to $9.30.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rocket Pharmaceuticals gets FDA RMAT status for heart disorder therapy
data.description:
• Rocket Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RCKT) said that the U.S. FDA has given regenerative medicine advanced therapy (RMAT) status to their gene therapy, RP-A601, for treating a heart condition known as PKP2-arrhythmogenic cardiomyopathy.
  • The stock is up 14% premarket.
  • The RMAT allows the company to have early discussions with the FDA about potential ways to measure the therapy's effectiveness, which could help speed up the approval process.

data.title: Wipro GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line, gross revenue of $2.58B beats by $10M; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Wipro press release (NYSE:WIT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.04 in-line.
  • Gross revenue of $2.58B (decrease of 1.6% Q/Q and increase of 0.8% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • IT services segment revenue was at $2,587.4 million, decrease of 0.3% QoQ and 1.5% YoY.
  • Total bookings was at $4,971 million, up by 24.1% QoQ and 50.7% YoY in constant currency. Large deal bookings was at $2,666 million, an increase of 49.7% QoQ and 130.8% YoY in constant currency.
  • Operating cash flows of ₹41.1 billion ($479.6 million), increase of 9.8% QoQ and 2.9% YoY.
  • Declared interim dividend of ₹5 ($0.061) per equity share/ADS.
  • Outlook for the Quarter ending September 30, 2025: "We expect revenue from our IT Services business segment to be in the range of $2,560 million to $2,612 million. This translates to sequential guidance of (-)1.0% to 1.0% in constant currency terms. "

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher buys Equinox Agency
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher (NYSE:AJG) has acquired Emmaus, Pennsylvania-based employee health and benefits consulting firm, Equinox Agency.
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed.

data.title: Plumas Bancorp declares $0.30 dividend
data.description:
• Plumas Bancorp (NASDAQ:PLBC) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.78%
  • Payable Aug. 15; for shareholders of record Aug. 1; ex-div Aug. 1.
  • See PLBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
